items.title: Add automated environment bootstrap for git worktrees
items.problem_statement: New worktrees lack .env, .secrets.yaml, installed dependencies (uv venv, node_modules), and MCP config. Agents cannot run code in worktrees, so they fall back to main and create merge conflicts. worktree.py setup needs a post-setup hook that copies .env from the main repo, runs uv sync, and configures the worktree as a runnable environment.
items.why_now: Root cause of agents abandoning worktrees and creating conflicts on main. Every parallel workflow session hits this. Small effort — extend worktree.py setup to call a bootstrap script.

items.title: Enable rerere, zdiff3, and merge queue for parallel-friendly git config
items.problem_statement: Git defaults are conservative for parallel development. rerere is disabled (recurring conflicts re-resolved manually), conflict markers lack base context (diff3/zdiff3 off), and no GitHub merge queue prevents broken main from concurrent PR merges. A .gitconfig overlay and CI merge_group trigger would fix all three.
items.why_now: Zero-downside improvements. rerere shares conflict resolutions across all worktrees automatically. zdiff3 gives agents better context for conflict resolution. GitHub merge queue prevents broken main. All are config-only changes.

items.title: Enable GitHub merge queue with merge_group CI trigger
items.problem_statement: When multiple agent PRs pass CI against the same main SHA and merge concurrently, main can break because PR B was never tested against PR A's changes. GitHub merge queue creates temporary branches combining all queued PRs and runs CI against the combined state before allowing merge.
items.why_now: Essential safety net for parallel PRs. Without it, the last-write-wins race condition on main is unsolved. Requires adding merge_group trigger to CI and enabling the queue in GitHub branch protection.

items.title: Reduce merge conflict surface through architectural separation
items.problem_statement: Multiple agents editing the same files (e.g., shared config, central routers, monolithic modules) creates merge conflicts even when changes are logically independent. Architectural patterns that reduce this: split large files into per-feature modules, use directory-based namespacing, adopt plugin/registry patterns for extensible systems, and move shared constants to dedicated files.
items.recommended_action: defer — identify hotspot files first, then plan targeted refactors
items.why_now: Structural root cause of merge conflicts. Port allocator and compose isolation already shipped, but code-level conflict surface remains. Need to analyze git blame/merge-conflict history to identify worst offenders.

items.title: Integrate coordinator merge queue with GitHub merge queue
items.problem_statement: The coordinator has a logical merge queue (merge_queue.py) with priority ordering and pre-merge resource conflict checks, but it operates independently from GitHub's merge queue. Integration would let the coordinator submit PRs to GitHub's queue in priority order, with pre-merge validation happening in the coordinator before GitHub CI runs.
items.recommended_action: defer — enable GitHub merge queue first, then integrate
items.why_now: Coordinator merge queue has priority ordering and resource-conflict awareness that GitHub's FIFO queue lacks. Combining both gives priority-ordered, CI-validated, conflict-aware merging.

items.title: Integrate port allocator into worktree lifecycle
items.problem_statement: The openspec-beads-worktree skill handles git worktree isolation but is unaware of port/resource isolation. Parallel task execution fails when tasks spin up services. Port allocator now exists but isn't wired into the worktree lifecycle.
items.recommended_action: defer — plan after worktree bootstrap is solid
items.why_now: Port allocator exists. Integration becomes relevant once worktrees are bootstrapped and agents actually run services in them.

items.title: Add git notes for agent provenance tracking
items.problem_statement: No lightweight mechanism to track which agent produced each commit, token usage, work-package membership, or validation status without polluting commit messages. Git notes (refs/notes/agent-metadata) with cat_sort_uniq merge strategy would provide post-hoc analysis capability.
items.recommended_action: defer — nice-to-have after core workflow improvements
items.why_now: Useful for analysis but not blocking any workflow. Coordinator audit trail already provides most of this data.
